about.name: Q1 2026 Health Check: How to Audit Your Wellness Goals and Stay on Track
At-home sample collection across Dubai and Abu Dhabi
Personalised wellness coaching from qualified dieticians and coaches
Integrated blood test packages with same-week results
Preventive health programmes designed for UAE lifestyle and climate

mainEntity.name: What is a Q1 health check?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Q1 health check is a focused 15-minute review of your wellness progress at the end of the first quarter, using goal-based and preventive health frameworks designed for UAE residents.

mainEntity.name: Why is quarterly goal review recommended?

acceptedAnswer.text: Quarterly goal reviews help people stay accountable, detect barriers early, and make timely adjustments for better health outcomes, as evidenced by peer-reviewed research.

mainEntity.name: What screenings are recommended in Dubai for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Dubai Health Authority recommends annual preventive health screenings including blood panels, metabolic markers, and cardiovascular risk assessments based on age and health status.

mainEntity.name: How can Dubai residents adjust routines after Ramadan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Gradually return to regular meal timings, focus on balanced nutrition, and adjust hydration to account for heat, following UAE Ministry guidelines.

mainEntity.name: What are easy ways to stay on track with wellness goals in the UAE?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use short quarterly reviews, align with DHA preventive guidance, adapt to season and cultural factors like Ramadan and summer, and use at-home screenings as needed.

mainEntity.name: Why should hydration be reviewed now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hydration needs increase with rising temperatures; MOHAP recommends improving water intake and adapting for the UAE climate to support metabolic and general health.

mainEntity.name: Does quarterly review help with behaviour change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, evidence from The Lancet shows regular self-review leads to higher adherence and more sustained health behaviour change compared to annual reviews.

@graph.headline: WHO — Promoting well-being and addressing the wider determinants of health
@graph.text: World Health Organization framework for holistic well-being, covering physical, mental, and social dimensions of health.

@graph.headline: Dubai Health Authority — Preventive Health Screenings
@graph.text: DHA guidelines on preventive screenings for UAE residents, including age-based recommendations for blood panels, metabolic markers, and cardiovascular risk.

@graph.headline: BMJ — Goal setting and review in clinical practice
@graph.text: Peer-reviewed evidence on quarterly goal review cadences in chronic-condition management; shorter review cycles correlate with better adherence and outcomes.

@graph.headline: UAE Ministry of Health and Prevention — Nutrition Guidelines for Adults
@graph.text: MOHAP's nutritional guidance tailored for UAE adults, accounting for regional dietary patterns and climate-related hydration needs.

@graph.headline: The Lancet — Behaviour change for health: evidence and challenges
@graph.text: Lancet review on the psychology of long-term behaviour change; identifies quarterly self-review as a key adherence mechanism.
